use crate::api::{self, StreamEvent, Usage};
use crate::config::Config;
use crate::message::{Message, Role};
use crate::storage::Storage;
use eframe::egui;
use egui_commonmark::{CommonMarkCache, CommonMarkViewer};
use tokio::sync::mpsc;
use tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken;

pub struct ChatApp {
    messages: Vec<Message>,
    input: String,
    base_url: String,
    models: Vec<String>,
    selected_model: usize,
    streaming: bool,
    rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>,
    /// Separate channel for model fetching so it doesn't conflict with chat streaming
    models_rx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<StreamEvent>>,
    cancel_token: Option<CancellationToken>,
    runtime: tokio::runtime::Runtime,
    models_loading: bool,
    error: Option<String>,
    system_prompt: String,
    temperature: f32,
    max_tokens: u32,
    use_max_tokens: bool,
    dark_mode: bool,
    commonmark_cache: CommonMarkCache,
    editing_message: Option<usize>,
    edit_buffer: String,
    show_settings: bool,
    // Persistence
    storage: Storage,
    current_conversation_id: Option<i64>,
    conversation_list: Vec<(i64, String)>,
    show_sidebar: bool,
    // Search
    search_query: String,
    search_results: Vec<(i64, String, String)>,
    show_search: bool,
    // Endpoints
    saved_endpoints: Vec<String>,
    show_endpoints: bool,
    new_endpoint: String,
    // Token usage
    last_usage: Option<Usage>,
    // Rename
    renaming_conversation: Option<i64>,
    rename_buffer: String,
    // Config
    config: Config,
}

impl ChatApp {
    pub fn new(cc: &eframe::CreationContext<'_>, config: Config) -> Self {
        configure_fonts(&cc.egui_ctx, &config);
        apply_theme(&cc.egui_ctx, config.dark_mode);
        cc.egui_ctx.set_pixels_per_point(config.ui_scale);

        let runtime = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().expect("Failed to create tokio runtime");
        let storage = Storage::new();
        let conversation_list = storage
            .list_conversations()
            .into_iter()
            .map(|c| (c.id, c.title))
            .collect();

        let base_url = config.default_endpoint.clone();

        let mut app = Self {
            messages: Vec::new(),
            input: String::new(),
            base_url: base_url.clone(),
            models: Vec::new(),
            selected_model: 0,
            streaming: false,
            rx: None,
            models_rx: None,
            cancel_token: None,
            runtime,
            models_loading: true,
            error: None,
            system_prompt: config.system_prompt.clone(),
            temperature: config.temperature,
            max_tokens: config.max_tokens,
            use_max_tokens: config.use_max_tokens,
            dark_mode: config.dark_mode,
            commonmark_cache: CommonMarkCache::default(),
            editing_message: None,
            edit_buffer: String::new(),
            show_settings: false,
            storage,
            current_conversation_id: None,
            conversation_list,
            show_sidebar: true,
            search_query: String::new(),
            search_results: Vec::new(),
            show_search: false,
            saved_endpoints: config.saved_endpoints.clone(),

## Task
Design an implementation plan for adding OpenRouter support to hChat, a Rust/egui desktop chat client that currently targets Ollama's OpenAI-compatible API.

## Current Architecture

### Files
- `src/api.rs` — API client. Has `fetch_models(base_url)` that hits Ollama's `/api/tags` endpoint, and `stream_chat()` that posts to `{base_url}/chat/completions` with SSE streaming. No auth headers.
- `src/app.rs` — Core UI. `ChatApp` struct holds state including `base_url`, `saved_endpoints: Vec<String>`, and a model selector dropdown. Has endpoint management UI (add/remove/switch endpoints).
- `src/config.rs` — TOML config at `~/.config/hchat/config.toml`. Has `default_endpoint`, `saved_endpoints: Vec<String>`, and various settings.
- `src/message.rs` — `Message` and `Role` types, OpenAI chat format compatible.
- `src/storage.rs` — SQLite persistence for conversations.
- `src/main.rs` — Entry point.

### Key observations
1. `stream_chat()` already uses OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` format — OpenRouter is compatible with this.
2. `fetch_models()` is hardcoded to Ollama's `/api/tags` — won't work with OpenRouter.
3. No API key / auth header support exists anywhere.
4. Endpoints are stored as simple strings (URLs) — no per-endpoint metadata.

### What OpenRouter needs
- API key in `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header
- Model listing via `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models` (returns OpenAI-style model list)
- Optional headers: `HTTP-Referer` and `X-Title` (for OpenRouter rankings)
- Uses standard OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` for chat

## Requirements
1. Support OpenRouter as an endpoint (user sets base URL to `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`)
2. Per-endpoint API key storage (some endpoints like localhost Ollama don't need keys, OpenRouter does)
3. Model fetching must work with both Ollama (`/api/tags`) and OpenAI-standard (`/v1/models`) endpoints
4. API keys should be passed as Bearer tokens in chat requests
5. Config should persist endpoint settings (URL + optional API key)
6. Keep it simple — don't over-architect a provider system

## Design constraints
- Rust with egui immediate-mode UI
- TOML config file at `~/.config/hchat/config.toml`
- Existing endpoint management UI should be extended, not replaced
- API keys are sensitive — consider whether they belong in the TOML config or elsewhere

## Please design
1. Data model changes (Config, ChatApp state)
2. API changes (auth headers, model fetching for OpenAI-standard endpoints)
3. UI changes (where to input/display API keys)
4. Migration path (existing configs should still work with serde defaults)
5. File-by-file change list with specifics

input.content:
# Plan: OpenRouter Support

## Context
hChat currently only works with Ollama (hardcoded `/api/tags` for model listing, no auth headers). OpenRouter and other OpenAI-compatible providers need: (1) API key in `Authorization: Bearer` header, (2) standard `/v1/models` endpoint for model listing. The streaming chat endpoint (`/v1/chat/completions`) already works since it's OpenAI-compatible.

## Approach
Extend endpoints from plain URL strings to structs with optional API key. Make model fetching work with both Ollama and OpenAI-standard APIs. No "provider type" selector — auto-detect via try-OpenAI-first-then-Ollama.

---

## Changes

### 1. `src/config.rs` — Endpoint struct + migration

- **Add `Endpoint` struct**: `{ url: String, api_key: Option<String> }`
- **Custom `Deserialize`** on `Endpoint` to accept either a plain string (`"http://..."`) or a table (`{ url = "...", api_key = "..." }`). This provides backward compat with existing configs.
- **Change** `Config::saved_endpoints` from `Vec<String>` to `Vec<Endpoint>`
- **Update** `Default for Config` and `sanitize()` accordingly
- Old configs auto-migrate on first load; on next save they write the new table format

### 2. `src/api.rs` — Auth headers + dual model fetching

- **Add** `OpenAIModel { id: String }` and `OpenAIModelsResponse { data: Vec<OpenAIModel> }` structs
- **`fetch_models(base_url, api_key: Option<&str>)`** — try `{base_url}/models` (OpenAI standard) first with auth header; if that fails, fall back to Ollama's `/api/tags`
- **`stream_chat(..., api_key: Option<String>)`** — add `.bearer_auth(key)` to the request when key is present
- **OpenRouter-specific**: when URL contains `openrouter.ai`, add `HTTP-Referer` and `X-Title` headers (recommended by their API docs)

### 3. `src/app.rs` — UI + wiring

- **Change** `saved_endpoints: Vec<String>` to `Vec<Endpoint>` (import from config)
- **Add fields**: `new_endpoint_key: String` (for add-endpoint form)
- **Add** `current_api_key() -> Option<&str>` helper that looks up API key by matching `self.base_url`
- **Update `fetch_models()`**: pass `api_key` to `api::fetch_models()`
- **Update `start_streaming()`**: pass `api_key` to `api::stream_chat()`
- **Endpoint management UI** (lines 574-609):
  - Add a password-masked API key field to the "Add endpoint" form
  - Show a key icon per endpoint in the list with ability to edit/set key
- **Endpoint selector ComboBox** (lines 524-537): iterate `.url` instead of plain strings
- **Save/Reset handlers** (lines 680-708): use `Vec<Endpoint>` type
- **Endpoint removal** (line 602): compare `removed.url` instead of `removed`

---

## Files unchanged
- `src/message.rs`, `src/storage.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `Cargo.toml` (no new deps needed)

## Implementation order
1. `config.rs` — Endpoint struct + custom deserialize + Config field changes
2. `api.rs` — add api_key params, dual model fetching, bearer auth
3. `app.rs` — wire it all together, update UI

## Verification
1. `cargo build` — confirms compilation
2. Test with Ollama (default): launch, verify models load and chat works as before
3. Test with OpenRouter: add `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` endpoint with API key, verify models populate and chat streams correctly
4. Test config migration: start with an old-format `config.toml`, verify it loads without error; save settings, verify it writes new format
5. Test no-key endpoint: confirm Ollama endpoints work fine without an API key

## Notes
- OpenRouter returns hundreds of models — the dropdown will be long. Model filtering/search is out of scope but worth noting as a follow-up.
- API keys in TOML config is the pragmatic choice for a local desktop app (same approach as Jan, Open WebUI, etc.)

## Approved Plan:
# Plan: OpenRouter Support

## Context
hChat currently only works with Ollama (hardcoded `/api/tags` for model listing, no auth headers). OpenRouter and other OpenAI-compatible providers need: (1) API key in `Authorization: Bearer` header, (2) standard `/v1/models` endpoint for model listing. The streaming chat endpoint (`/v1/chat/completions`) already works since it's OpenAI-compatible.

## Approach
Extend endpoints from plain URL strings to structs with optional API key. Make model fetching work with both Ollama and OpenAI-standard APIs. No "provider type" selector — auto-detect via try-OpenAI-first-then-Ollama.

---

## Changes

### 1. `src/config.rs` — Endpoint struct + migration

- **Add `Endpoint` struct**: `{ url: String, api_key: Option<String> }`
- **Custom `Deserialize`** on `Endpoint` to accept either a plain string (`"http://..."`) or a table (`{ url = "...", api_key = "..." }`). This provides backward compat with existing configs.
- **Change** `Config::saved_endpoints` from `Vec<String>` to `Vec<Endpoint>`
- **Update** `Default for Config` and `sanitize()` accordingly
- Old configs auto-migrate on first load; on next save they write the new table format

### 2. `src/api.rs` — Auth headers + dual model fetching

- **Add** `OpenAIModel { id: String }` and `OpenAIModelsResponse { data: Vec<OpenAIModel> }` structs
- **`fetch_models(base_url, api_key: Option<&str>)`** — try `{base_url}/models` (OpenAI standard) first with auth header; if that fails, fall back to Ollama's `/api/tags`
- **`stream_chat(..., api_key: Option<String>)`** — add `.bearer_auth(key)` to the request when key is present
- **OpenRouter-specific**: when URL contains `openrouter.ai`, add `HTTP-Referer` and `X-Title` headers (recommended by their API docs)

### 3. `src/app.rs` — UI + wiring

- **Change** `saved_endpoints: Vec<String>` to `Vec<Endpoint>` (import from config)
- **Add fields**: `new_endpoint_key: String` (for add-endpoint form)
- **Add** `current_api_key() -> Option<&str>` helper that looks up API key by matching `self.base_url`
- **Update `fetch_models()`**: pass `api_key` to `api::fetch_models()`
- **Update `start_streaming()`**: pass `api_key` to `api::stream_chat()`
- **Endpoint management UI** (lines 574-609):
  - Add a password-masked API key field to the "Add endpoint" form
  - Show a key icon per endpoint in the list with ability to edit/set key
- **Endpoint selector ComboBox** (lines 524-537): iterate `.url` instead of plain strings
- **Save/Reset handlers** (lines 680-708): use `Vec<Endpoint>` type
- **Endpoint removal** (line 602): compare `removed.url` instead of `removed`

---

## Files unchanged
- `src/message.rs`, `src/storage.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `Cargo.toml` (no new deps needed)

## Implementation order
1. `config.rs` — Endpoint struct + custom deserialize + Config field changes
2. `api.rs` — add api_key params, dual model fetching, bearer auth
3. `app.rs` — wire it all together, update UI

## Verification
1. `cargo build` — confirms compilation
2. Test with Ollama (default): launch, verify models load and chat works as before
3. Test with OpenRouter: add `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` endpoint with API key, verify models populate and chat streams correctly
4. Test config migration: start with an old-format `config.toml`, verify it loads without error; save settings, verify it writes new format
5. Test no-key endpoint: confirm Ollama endpoints work fine without an API key

## Notes
- OpenRouter returns hundreds of models — the dropdown will be long. Model filtering/search is out of scope but worth noting as a follow-up.
- API keys in TOML config is the pragmatic choice for a local desktop app (same approach as Jan, Open WebUI, etc.)

**`src/config.rs`**
- Added `Endpoint` struct with `url` and optional `api_key`

- `fetch_models()` now tries OpenAI-standard `/v1/models` first, falls back to Ollama `/api/tags`

- Bearer auth header added when API key is present
- OpenRouter-specific `HTTP-Referer` and `X-Title` headers when URL contains `openrouter.ai`

**`src/app.rs`**
- `saved_endpoints` field changed to `Vec<Endpoint>`
- Added `current_api_key()` helper to look up key for active endpoint
- API key passed through to `fetch_models()` and `stream_chat()`
- Endpoint management UI now includes a password-masked API key field when adding endpoints
- Key icon per endpoint shows whether an API key is set (click to clear)
- Endpoint selector ComboBox updated to work with `Endpoint` structs

content.text:
## UI flow

1. Click the **`+`** button next to the endpoint selector in the top bar — this opens the endpoint manager panel
2. In the "Add endpoint" row, type `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` in the URL field and paste your OpenRouter API key in the **API key** field (it's password-masked)
3. Click **Add**
4. The new endpoint appears in the endpoint selector dropdown — select it
5. Models auto-fetch from OpenRouter using your key
6. Click **Save settings** to persist

## Config file

After saving, your `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` changes from the old flat format:

```toml
saved_endpoints = ["http://localhost:11434/v1"]
```

To the new table-array format:

```toml
default_endpoint = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"

[[saved_endpoints]]
url = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

[[saved_endpoints]]
url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
api_key = "sk-or-v1-..."
```

You can also edit the TOML directly — the app reads it on launch. The old string format still works (endpoints without keys just get `api_key: None`).

## Switching back

Just pick the Ollama endpoint from the dropdown. No key needed for localhost, so it works as before. The `default_endpoint` field controls which is active on launch — it updates when you hit Save settings.

One thing worth noting: OpenRouter returns hundreds of models, so that dropdown will be long. A model search/filter would be a good follow-up if that gets annoying.

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [Ollama](https://ollama.com) at `localhost:11434`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- Markdown rendering in AI responses (code blocks, bold, lists)
- Model selector auto-populated from Ollama
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, and export
- System prompt, temperature, and max tokens settings
- Multiple saved API endpoints
- Edit and resend previous messages
- Copy button on messages
- Token usage display
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`)
- Configurable font sizes and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

Make sure your local LLM server is running first:

```bash
ollama serve
```

Then launch hChat:

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:11434/v1` by default. You can change the endpoint in the top bar.

## Configuration

Settings are stored in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` and persist across sessions. You can edit the file directly or use the settings panel in the app (gear icon).

```toml
font_family = "JetBrains Mono"
mono_font_family = "JetBrains Mono"
font_size = 14.0
mono_font_size = 13.0
ui_scale = 1.0
dark_mode = true
default_endpoint = "http://localhost:11434/v1"
system_prompt = ""
temperature = 0.7
max_tokens = 2048
use_max_tokens = false
saved_endpoints = ["http://localhost:11434/v1"]
```

All fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

### Custom fonts

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl+N | New conversation |

# hChat

A lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs. Built in Rust with [egui](https://github.com/emilk/egui) for a minimal, fast UI.

Connects to any OpenAI API-compatible endpoint. Defaults to [Ollama](https://ollama.com) at `localhost:11434`.

## Features

- Streaming token display with stop/regenerate controls
- Markdown rendering in AI responses (code blocks, bold, lists)
- Model selector auto-populated from your endpoint
- Conversation history with SQLite persistence
- Sidebar with conversation list, search, and export
- System prompt, temperature, and max tokens settings
- Multiple saved API endpoints
- Edit and resend previous messages
- Copy button on messages
- Token usage display
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Persistent settings via TOML config (`~/.config/hchat/config.toml`)
- Configurable font sizes and UI scale
- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS)

## Install

Download the latest release from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat/releases).

### macOS

```bash
# Binary
tar xzf hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz
mv hchat /usr/local/bin/

# Or use the .app bundle
unzip hChat.app.zip -d /Applications
```

### Debian/Ubuntu

```bash
sudo dpkg -i hchat_*.deb
```

### Arch Linux

```bash
# From the repo's pkg/arch directory
makepkg -si
```

## Build from source

Requires the [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs).

```bash
cargo run --release
```

## Launch

Make sure your local LLM server is running first:

```bash
ollama serve
```

Then launch hChat:

```bash
# Linux: run detached so it doesn't tie up your terminal
hchat &disown

# macOS: open the .app bundle, or run detached
open /Applications/hChat.app
# or
hchat &disown
```

hChat connects to `http://localhost:11434/v1` by default. You can change the endpoint in the top bar.

## Configuration

Settings are stored in `~/.config/hchat/config.toml` and persist across sessions. You can edit the file directly or use the settings panel in the app (gear icon).

```toml
font_family = "JetBrains Mono"
mono_font_family = "JetBrains Mono"
font_size = 14.0
mono_font_size = 13.0
ui_scale = 1.0
dark_mode = true
default_endpoint = "http://localhost:11434/v1"
system_prompt = ""
temperature = 0.7
max_tokens = 2048
use_max_tokens = false
saved_endpoints = ["http://localhost:11434/v1"]
```

All fields are optional. Missing fields use defaults, so existing configs won't break on upgrade.

### Custom fonts

Set `font_family` and `mono_font_family` to any font installed on your system. hChat looks up fonts by name using your platform's font system (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS). Leave empty to use egui's built-in fonts. Font changes take effect on save and restart.

## Keybindings

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| Enter | Send message |
| Shift+Enter | New line |
| Ctrl+N | New conversation |

input.content:
# hChat configuration
# Copy to ~/.config/hchat/config.toml and edit to your liking.
# All fields are optional — missing fields use the defaults shown here.

# --- Appearance ---

# Dark mode (true) or light mode (false)
dark_mode = true

# UI scale factor (0.75 - 2.0)
ui_scale = 1.0

# Proportional font for UI text. Leave empty for egui built-in font.
# Uses your system's installed fonts (fontconfig on Linux, Core Text on macOS).
font_family = ""

# Monospace font for code blocks. Leave empty for egui built-in font.
mono_font_family = ""

# Font sizes (8.0 - 24.0)
font_size = 14.0
mono_font_size = 13.0

# --- Model settings ---

# System prompt prepended to every conversation
system_prompt = ""

# Sampling temperature (0.0 - 2.0)
temperature = 0.7

# Max tokens for model responses (64 - 16384)
max_tokens = 2048

# Whether to send max_tokens to the API. When false, the model decides.
use_max_tokens = false

# --- Endpoints ---

# The active endpoint on launch (must match a url in saved_endpoints)
default_endpoint = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

# Saved endpoints. Each entry has a url and an optional api_key.
# Endpoints without an api_key don't send an Authorization header (e.g. local Ollama).

# Local Ollama (no key needed)
[[saved_endpoints]]
url = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

# OpenRouter (requires API key from https://openrouter.ai/keys)
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
# api_key = "sk-or-v1-your-key-here"

# LM Studio (typically no key needed)
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "http://localhost:1234/v1"

# Any OpenAI-compatible API
# [[saved_endpoints]]
# url = "https://api.example.com/v1"
# api_key = "your-key-here"
